Sacramento, CA95804
Dear Mr. Wallace:
I am writing per our conversatioD to obtain a formal FPPC ruling on whether
or not I have a conflict of interest on an upcoming San Carlos City Council
issue. As you will recall, I serve on the San Carlos Council and am employed
full time by PG&E.
The Council will consider on May 29 a motion to place oo the November 1990
ballot a utility users tax. This tax is noted s€perately on utility bills. It is
collected by PG&E (and other impacted utilities) aod then disbursed to the
cities. PG&E incurs no administrative costs if cities use tie attached model
ordinance enacting a users tax with no exemptions or special conditions. If
low income seniors are exempted and fees are capped at $10,000 (such as has
been proposed in San Carlos), then PG&E does incur somo programming costs.
While the exact cost is not ayailable, it is certainly less than the $250,000
expense mentioned in the attached regulation 18702.2(2).
My imprcssion from reading this regulation and from talkitrg with the City
Attomey and a PG&E attomey is that I do not have a conflict of interest since
t}te tax would not have a direct, material or significant impact on PG&E. I
would like this confirmcd or denied by the FPPC so lhat I can act or abstain
with some degree of certainty.
By copy of this letter, I am asking the City Attorney and PG&E attorney to
forward their opinions in writing to me by May 16. I will mail these opinions
to you to assist itr making a ruling.
Since the Council has scheduled a vol.e on this issue on May 29, I would
appreciate a written opinion no later than Mry 25. If this is not sufficient
time, please communicate a formal opinion verbally.
Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Dear Mr. Wallace:
Per my letter of May 10, I am forwarding a written opinion I received from
the San Carlos City Attomey regarding my potential conflict of interest on a
utility users tax vote before the San Carlos City Council.
I still am lacking three pieces of information:
* 
The FPPC ruling on whether I have a conflict of interest.
+ 
Whether the cosl trigger for direct material impact to PG&E is
$250,000 or $100,000.
* 
The administrative cost to PG&E of implementing this tax.
I am trying to find the answer to the last two questions from PG&E personnel.
Any information I receive will be sent on to you. I appreciate your help on
the first item, and I hope the attached witl be of assistance.
S incerely,

CITY ATTORNEY
POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST
Dear Paul:
In your letter of Uay 10, 1990, to the FPPe you asked,
indirectly, for my opinion as City Attorney on your potential
conflict of interest in voting on the issue of a Utility user Tax.
My understandinq is that you are an employee of Pacific Gas &
Electric, which is a utility 
comPany doing business in the city of
San carlos.
Under Government Code 587103, subparagraph (d), there is
no question that you have a prima facie conflict of interest in
that you are an enployee of a utiLity conpany that is indirectly
affected by the decision on the utility user tax. The question is
whether the decision will have a naterial financial effect
distinguishable fron its effect on the public Aenerally. You did
include with your naterial a copy of FPPC Regulation S18702.2 Irhich
deals with business entities indirectly invoLved or affected by a
decisj.=n. Ilnde= that code S=cticn a business entity such as PG&E
that incurs expenses through utility 
user tax in the amount of
$1o0,000. or more would certainly be naterially affected. I an not
sure if that is the correct subparagraph that appJ-ies to Pc&E on
the sliding scale set forth in the regulations.
There renains an issue as to whether or not such a
decision is held to have an effect your position and chance of
promotion within PG&E. If there would be such an effect it night
be held to be material.
Finally, it seems to ne that this nust have been
for an elected official, 
who rras an enployee of a utility
in one of the other jurisdictions in caliiornia that hav3
decided
company
adopted


Mr. Paul Siv1ey

a utility user tax. This cannot be the first time it has occurred.
I realize tne urgency of your having to find out the answer to this
question. We wiff foffow through with the FPPC to see if, in fact,
€trey have written an opinion letter on this very issue before. I
wou-Id think the chances of their having done that are very good-
If you have any questions
